session_bias.reasoning: NVDA SELL-THE-NEWS CONFIRMED (21:15 ET 5/20). NVDA beat massively (EPS $1.87 vs $1.78, Revenue $81.62B vs $79.2B, Q2 guidance $91B, raised dividend 25x) but stock declined AH: $223.33 close -> ~$220.40 AH (-1.31% to -2.83%). Classic sell-the-news on the most anticipated earnings. NVDA ~5.5% SPY weight. Combined with: (1) SPY AH already at 737.82 (-$3.47 from 741.29 close), (2) 738.10 Carry rejection confirmed in AH (HIGH=738.129, CLOSE below), (3) BEAR ribbon in AH (Fast 738.06 < Pivot 738.66 < Slow 739.19, spread 113c). BIAS TILTS BEARISH. Most likely scenario: SPY opens near 737-738, any rally to 738.10 gets rejected. PREMARKET must verify with live chart at 08:30 ET — overnight bid recovery possible.
session_bias.key_scenario_bear: Open at/below 738.10 (likely given AH at 737.82 + NVDA sell-the-news) -> rally to 738.10 -> bar closes below -> ribbon BEAR confirmed -> ENTER short. Target 735.40 (*** Active support). Runner to 733.77. HS watcher prime candidate for first live win here.
session_bias.key_scenario_bull: NVDA recovers in premarket above $225 AND SPY gaps above 738.10 AND holds 738.10 as support -> BULL enter, target 741.29/741.61. LOW PROBABILITY given extent of sell-the-news. Would require institutional buyers stepping in AH overnight.
session_bias.invalidation_bear: SPY opens above 738.86 (flipped resistance) and holds for 2+ bars with vol >= 1.2x avg -- means overnight buyers absorbed the NVDA selling
session_bias.invalidation_bull: Break and close below 735.40 *** support with vol >= 2x avg within first 60 min
session_bias.nvda_catalyst_note: NVDA AH reaction is the primary driver. Premarket task MUST check NVDA premarket price. If NVDA recovers above $225+ in premarket, SPY bias should be re-evaluated toward CONDITIONAL. If NVDA stays below $222, bear thesis stays ACTIVE.
session_bias.close_ceiling_alert: 738.10 IS the close-ceiling level (L59 pattern). If first bar opens near 738 and tests 738.10 but fails -> N=1. Wait for N>=3 before CLOSE_CEILING_DISTRIBUTION_FADE signal fires. Track each bar carefully in first 60 min.
